summary:
Whole genome sequencing has limited utility in diagnosing mitochondrial diseases due to poor detection of low-level heteroplasmic mutations and high rates of variants classified as uncertain significance. Targeted mtDNA tests remain more reliable and clinically validated than WGS for identifying causative pathogenic variants. 

The summary should reflect only what was stated directly within Agent2’s response—no external additions or interpretations beyond those explicitly made.

Whole genome sequencing is ineffective for diagnosing mitochondrial diseases due to inadequate detection of low-level heteroplasmic mutations and frequent identification of variants of uncertain significance. Targeted mtDNA testing provides greater reliability and clinical validity compared to WGS for confirming pathogenic variants.
